rulings.Calendar Matter: Paul Sack'S Notice Of Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, September 5, 2024, Line 1. 1 - PETITIONER PAUL SACK's Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award. Petitioner Sack's ("Sack") petition to vacate arbitration award is denied and petitioner San Francisco Museum of Modern Art ("SFMOMA") petition to confirm the arbitration award is granted. Sack seeks to vacate the "DECISION FOLLOWING PARTIES' REQUEST FOR HEARING ON SUBMISSIONS REFERENCING SFMOMA'S YEAR-END 2023 ANNUAL REPORTING" ("Decision") that the arbitrator issued on March 25, 2024. But the Decision is not a final award that can be vacated under the California Arbitration Act. Under CCP 1283.4, an "award" determines "all the questions submitted to the arbitrators the decision of which is necessary in order to determine the controversy." Here, the arbitrator issued the CCP 1283.4 award ("FINAL RULING ON CLAIMANT'S MARCH 2, 2022 DEMAND") on October 30, 2022. Therefore, Sack's oblique attempt to vacate the final award through the Decision is untimely per CCP 1288 [100-day period to vacate award]. The ensuant Decision merely clarified the final award. The Decision ruled that SFMOMA's report complied with the reporting requirements of the "Final Ruling" and "reiterates" that SFMOMA did not breach the parties' settlement agreement. (Petition to Vacate, Attachment 8(c), pg. 4.) The text of the "Final Ruling" and Decision make clear that the "Final Ruling" is the award. In sum, the epilogue Decision did not restart the clock to attack the final award and Sack's attempt to do so now is untimely. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ANN MARIE URBANSKI VS. EPSILON DATA MANAGEMENT, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, September 5, 2024, Line 8. PLAINTIFF ANN URBANSKI's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND Amended COMPLAINT. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file third amended complaint is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP 428.50(c).) A proposed complaint's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) The trial date is December 16, 2024. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
